div.p: Invariant: Committed entries are guaranteed to be present in all future leaders.
The overlap ensures that any node elected as leader must have communicated with at least one node containing the latest committed log entry.
1. Majority Persistence Entry E is committed on majority M1. $|M_1| \ge 3$ nodes possess E.
2. Election Requirement New leader L wins via majority M2. L must receive votes from $\ge 3$ nodes.
3. Quorum Intersection By Pigeonhole Principle: M1 ∩ M2 ≠ ∅. Node C exists in both sets.
4. Log Comparison Rule C only votes for L if $Log_L \ge Log_C$. Since C has E, L must also have E.
Theorem Proved Entry E is guaranteed in Leader L's log.
